q1_safety.text: What should you do before starting a cleanup event?

options.q1_b: Assess the site for hazards, prepare safety gear, and brief volunteers
options.q1_d: Wait for volunteers to figure it out themselves

q2_moderation.text: What happens after you create a cleanup event?

options.q2_a: It is immediately visible to all volunteers
options.q2_b: Moderators review it before volunteers can see and join it
options.q2_d: Only you can see it forever

q3_checkin.text: How do you verify that a volunteer actually attended your event?

options.q3_b: Ask them to send a selfie
options.q3_c: Use the in-app QR check-in system so attendance is verified on-site

q4_weather.text: If dangerous weather is forecast for your cleanup window, what is the best practice?

options.q4_a: Ignore the forecast and go ahead
options.q4_b: Cancel or postpone and clearly tell registered volunteers in the app
options.q4_d: Assume everyone will check the news themselves

q5_waste.text: When sorting collected waste, what should organizers prioritize?

options.q5_a: Mix everything in one bag to save time
options.q5_b: Follow local guidance: separate recyclables and dispose at proper facilities when possible
options.q5_c: Leave bags at the site for someone else
options.q5_d: Burn waste if it is faster

q6_inclusion.text: How should you welcome volunteers with different abilities or backgrounds?

options.q6_a: Assign only the hardest tasks to new people
options.q6_b: Offer clear roles, reasonable pacing, and ask how you can support participation
options.q6_c: Assume everyone can lift the same weight
options.q6_d: Skip the safety briefing to save time

q7_privacy.text: What is the safest approach to personal contact details in the public event chat?

options.q7_a: Post your phone number so everyone can call you
options.q7_b: Ask volunteers to post their emails publicly
options.q7_c: Keep sensitive personal data out of public chat; use official in-app channels
options.q7_d: Share home addresses for carpooling without asking

q8_evidence.text: Why upload honest “after” cleanup photos in the app?

options.q8_a: They are optional and never used
options.q8_b: They help the community see real impact and keep the platform trustworthy
options.q8_c: Only moderators see photos, so any image is fine

q9_emergency.text: If someone is injured during a cleanup, what should the organizer do first?

options.q9_a: Continue the event and deal with it later
options.q9_b: Stop unsafe work, provide basic first aid if trained, and call local emergency services when needed
options.q9_c: Ask volunteers to diagnose injuries online
options.q9_d: Avoid calling help to prevent attention

q10_capacity.text: If your event reaches the maximum participants set in the app, what should you do?

options.q10_a: Let extra people join unofficially anyway
options.q10_b: Respect the limit for safety and logistics; offer a waitlist or schedule another date
options.q10_c: Lower the limit without telling anyone
options.q10_d: Charge money at the gate for entry

q11_declined.text: If moderators decline your event, what can you typically do next?

options.q11_a: Create duplicate events until one is approved
options.q11_b: Read the feedback, fix the issues, and resubmit through the app
options.q11_d: Ignore the decline and advertise off-platform only

q12_disposal.text: After the cleanup, what is the best practice for collected waste?

options.q12_a: Leave bags hidden at the site
options.q12_b: Take waste to authorized disposal or recycling points following local rules
options.q12_c: Burn mixed waste on-site if it is cold
options.q12_d: Dump in any river to wash it away

q13_meeting.text: How should you choose the meeting point for volunteers?

options.q13_a: Pick a random spot each time without a map pin
options.q13_b: Use a clear, reachable location tied to the event site and describe access in the description
options.q13_c: Share only voice directions in private messages
options.q13_d: Ask people to wander until they find you

q14_minors.text: When families bring children to a cleanup, what is the organizer’s responsibility?

options.q14_a: Organizers become legal guardians of every child
options.q14_b: Ensure activities match the stated difficulty; remind parents/guardians they supervise their children
options.q14_c: Assign children to work alone far from adults
options.q14_d: Children are not allowed at cleanups

q15_updates.text: If the start time or meeting place changes last minute, what should you do?

options.q15_a: Assume everyone will notice the change automatically
options.q15_b: Update the event in the app and post a clear note in event chat so joined volunteers are informed
options.q15_c: Only tell people you know personally

q16_integrity.text: Why is it important to report accurate bag counts and attendance?

options.q16_a: Inflated numbers look better for social media only
options.q16_b: Accurate data keeps credits fair, helps funders trust outcomes, and improves planning
options.q16_d: Only organizers see totals, so accuracy does not matter

q17_qr_rotate.text: If a check-in QR code may have been shared or photographed outside your group, what should you do?

options.q17_a: Ignore it; QR codes never expire
options.q17_b: Use the organizer check-in tools to rotate the session so new scans require a fresh QR
options.q17_c: Turn off the internet at the site
options.q17_d: Ask attendees to uninstall the app

q18_moderators.text: Where should you go first if you need help with moderation or a serious safety concern on the platform?

options.q18_a: Publicly name and shame other users in chat
options.q18_b: Use official in-app reporting and moderator channels rather than escalating in public posts
options.q18_c: Share private screenshots on social media
options.q18_d: Delete the event to hide the problem
